items.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes several inconsistencies found by reviewing the `documentation/` folder against the actual source code and repository structure.

---

### Changes Made

#### 1. Node.js version requirements (`Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md`, `Generate-code-from-TypeSpec.md`, `migrating-to-typespec.md`)

**Problem:** Three documentation files referred to "LTS versions of Node.js" (without specifying a minimum), while:
- The root `package.json` declares `"node": ">=20"`
- `DPG-quickstart.md` and `RLC-quickstart.md` already correctly say "Node.js 20 or later"

**Fix:** Updated all three files to say "Node.js 20 or later" (linking to the official download page), making them consistent with the minimum version requirement and the other quickstart guides.

---

#### 2. `npm run` → `pnpm run` in test commands (`golden-testing-commands.md`)

**Problem:** Six test command examples used `npm run` (e.g. `npm run test:node`, `npm run integration-test:node`) while the entire repository uses `pnpm` as the package manager and all other docs use `pnpm run`.

**Fix:** Replaced all six occurrences with `pnpm run`.

---

#### 3. Deprecated `customization apply-v2` command (`migrating-to-typespec.md`)

**Problem:** The migration guide referred to `dev-tool run customization apply-v2` and `npx dev-tool customization apply-v2` in two places. The `apply-v2` subcommand in `common/tools/dev-tool/src/commands/customization/apply-v2.ts` now emits:
> ⚠️  'dev-tool customization apply-v2' is deprecated and will be removed soon. Use 'dev-tool customization apply' instead.

**Fix:** Replaced both occurrences with `dev-tool run customization apply` / `npx dev-tool customization apply`.

---

#### 4. Stale `InteractiveBrowserCredential` note (`using-azure-identity.md`)

**Problem:** The guide contained a note saying:
> NOTE: At this time, this credential can only be used in the browser but Node.js support will be added in the future (see issue #4774).

Node.js support has long since been added — `sdk/identity/identity/src/credentials/interactiveBrowserCredential.ts` (the Node.js implementation) exists in the repo and `InteractiveBrowserCredentialNodeOptions` is exported.

**Fix:** Removed the stale note and updated the description to mention that Node.js is supported via the authorization code flow with PKCE.

---

### Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `documentation/Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md` | Node.js prereq: "any LTS" → "20 or later" |
| `documentation/Generate-code-from-TypeSpec.md` | Node.js prereq: "LTS versions" → "20 or later" |
| `documentation/migrating-to-typespec.md` | Node.js prereq + `apply-v2` → `apply` |
| `documentation/golden-testing-commands.md` | `npm run` → `pnpm run` (6 occurrences) |
| `documentation/using-azure-identity.md` | Remove outdated Node.js-not-supported note |
items.title: docs: fix documentation inconsistencies across multiple files
